iteration_1_baseline.notes: Original verbose skill. Model did not load skill. Log-analyze failed: wrong commit count (wc -l bug), wrong files_changed (empty lines counted), swapped dates (head/tail confusion).

iteration_2.notes: Rewrote as procedural guide with exact commands and pitfall warnings. Model loaded skill. Log-analyze fixed. Branch-ops broke: skill too long, model lost tool-calling ability after loading skill (output raw JSON as text instead of tool calls).

iteration_3.notes: Shortened skill significantly (compact table format). Branch-ops recovered. Log-analyze regressed: model collected all correct values but hallucinated writing the file (never called write_file).

iteration_4.notes: Added explicit reminder to use write_file tool. Log-analyze fixed. Branch-ops partial: model named branch 'release-0.9' instead of 'release-1.0' (confused tag name with branch name from prompt). Random SLM error.

iteration_5_final.notes: Same skill as iteration 4. Both tasks pass. Branch naming error was non-deterministic.

changes_made: Replaced generic 432-line reference guide with compact 40-line procedural skill
Added exact commands in table format for all git history analysis operations
Fixed commit counting: git rev-list --count HEAD instead of wc -l with format strings
Fixed file counting: added grep -v '^$' to filter empty lines
Fixed date ordering: explicit tail -1 for earliest, head -1 for latest with explanation
Fixed merge counting: git rev-list --merges HEAD
Added --all flag reminder for cross-branch commit searches
Added explicit write_file reminder to prevent hallucinated file creation
Made description more assertive to trigger skill loading
Kept skill short (~40 lines) to avoid overwhelming qwen3-30b context
